title: Modular Arithmetic and Public-Key Reasoning · Runtime Contract: Crypto Go Toy Math Separation
summary: An implementation team manipulates big integers without normalizing signs, confuses modular inverse with division, and mistakes a worked toy example for secure key generation. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the toy math separation increment in bounded modular arithmetic reasoning notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Keep small-number demonstrations outside production code and name the parameter, randomness, validation, and side-channel evidence they omit.
